title: Emit array and struct info in FST
body:
Draft for discussion. Utilize new FST arrayscope type (gtkwave/libfst#17)

Old GTKWave can open the files. Displays a little odd but usable.

Old Surfer crashes but Surfer is generally faster to move so can probably depend on an update there (thanks @oscargus).

Please see the updated test_regress/t/t_trace_complex_structs_cc_fst.out for how the scope tree looks like (@ekiwi I think this is what you asked for, but there are a couple additional attributes on both structs and arrays now to convey packed/unpackedness and the number of members/left-right indices, these currently also might crash wellen?)

One kink we have in Verilator is that we depend on `apt install` for `fst2vcd`, which uses the older libfst, so the new FST_ST_SV_ARRAY scopes come out "module" as a fallback. This means if we ever pull a new `fst2vcd` the test references will need updating. Should we just build `fst2vcd` ourselves to always match our version of the lib?

---

- Emit arrays under the new FST scope type FST_ST_SV_ARRAY
- Emit packed/unpacked 'array' attributes before FST_ST_SV_ARRAY, including left and right indices of the array
- Emit packed/unpacked 'pack' attributes before FST_ST_VCD_STRUCT and FST_ST_VCD_UNION, including the number of members in the types

All attributes apply to the immediately following scope definition (just like enum dtype attributes).
